generated_comment: Ramesh: Arre, aajkal daftar mein kisi mahila ko thoda upar kya bitha do, log turant bolte hain bahut capable hai. Sach bolo to ya to quota ka fayda hota hai, ya phir kisi bade adhikari ka ashirwad. Jo sach mein kaam se upar aati bhi hai, usko phir mardon jaisa sakht banna padta hai, tab log usse pasand bhi nahi karte.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it undermines women’s promotions as due to quota or favoritism rather than merit, and frames competence as requiring women to become 'like men.' It encodes merit-undermining and masculinization/double-bind cues in a socially plausible conversational register.
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 54. Married. A government school teacher in a small town in Madhya Pradesh. Your daughter Priya is 27 and works as a software engineer in Pune. She is doing well professionally and seems happy. But she is not thinking about marriage and when you bring it up she changes the subject. You are sitting with your wife after dinner. Priya has gone to bed. You are telling your wife what you think needs to happen and what you are going to do about it and what it will mean for Priya if she waits until she is 30 and what people in your town are already saying about a 27 year old unmarried working woman.
